source: Learning Ansible with Rocky - Ansible Basics: Client preparation and SSH key authentication

questions.q: Which group is the dedicated ansible user added to so it can use sudo?
questions.why: usermod -aG wheel ansible; members of wheel are granted sudo rights.

questions.q: Why set NOPASSWD for the wheel group in sudoers during this setup?
options: So Ansible can run privileged tasks without a sudo password prompt
To allow root login over SSH
To speed up the SSH handshake
questions.why: The %wheel NOPASSWD: ALL line lets wheel members sudo without a password; otherwise you get "Missing sudo password".

questions.q: You hit: "to use the 'ssh' connection type with passwords, you must install the sshpass program". The fix?
options: Install sshpass on the management station
Add the host to the inventory again
questions.why: sudo dnf install sshpass provides the helper needed for password-based SSH.

questions.q: By default Ansible forbids password login. Which [defaults] setting in ansible.cfg enables the password prompt?
questions.why: Uncomment ask_pass in the [defaults] section and set it to True.

questions.q: Generate an SSH key pair for the ansible user.
questions.why: ssh-keygen creates the private/public RSA key pair used for key authentication.

questions.q: Copy the ansible user's public key to the host 172.16.1.10.
questions.why: ssh-copy-id installs the public key on the target so key-based login works.

questions.q: Why does the guide replace password authentication with SSH keys?
options: Key-based auth is much more secure than sending passwords
questions.why: Being prompted for the remote password is a security problem; private/public keys are preferred.

questions.q: Once key authentication works, what should you do in production?
options: Re-comment ask_pass and remove the ansible account passwords
Store the passwords in the inventory
Email the passwords to each host
questions.why: With keys working, a login password is no longer needed; remove it to harden security.
